name: How long do I have to sue Perplexity after my content shows up in their AI answers?
description: 3 years for copyright from discovery; act sooner to avoid defenses like laches.[1]

mainEntity.name: How long do I have to sue Perplexity after my content shows up in their AI answers?

acceptedAnswer.text: 3 years for copyright from discovery; act sooner to avoid defenses like laches.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: You generally have three years from discovering the infringement to file a copyright claim under U.S. law.[1] For trademarks, four years from harm accrual applies in many jurisdictions.[1] The NYT suit was filed promptly after scraping detection, emphasizing timely action to avoid laches defenses.[1] Key factors: accrual date (discovery), ongoing infringement tolling, and jurisdiction nuances like NY's six-year contract limit if applicable.[1] Track all instances continuously; file before evidence degrades or defenses strengthen.
